description.value: Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.
Used for rolling to hit when fighting in ranged or melee combat, in place of any other skills. Does not use a modifier.
Used for rolling checks involving feats of physicality, including athletics and evasion. Does not use a modifier.
Used for rolling when making checks that require training or finesse. Note: if the task is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers accordingly. Does not use a modifier.
Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.
Medium version of an Armorjack for your head. -2 to REF, DEX, and MOVE.
Medium version of an Armorjack. -2 to REF, DEX, and MOVE.

Has been modified to spray chemicals instead of shotgun shells (same area of effect; loaded with Vials of Biotoxin, Poison, or other aerosolized drugs; 1 dose of a drug = 4 shots, the target rolls resist drugs/torture instead of evasion when determining its effect after firing)
3d6 damage unless Resist Torture/Drugs. Armor does not apply.

ReTox is produced by the Bozo Circus of Merriment. It takes the form of tablets that look similar to jelly beans or other candy, and overpowers the user’s mouth with flavor (usually fruit or cinnamon) when bit into. The drug is impossibly addictive, to the point that even partygangs will hesitate to take it despite its potent effects. ReTox becomes one of the only things that an addict is able to taste, after heavy usage.

Primary Effect

  • Dose lasts 20 minutes.

  • For the duration of the primary effect, the user gains crit successes on 9s, and crit fails on 2s.

  • For the duration of the primary effect, you veer between an induced state of euphoria and heightened sensory input (rolls of 6+), and severe depression (rolls of 1-5), and struggle to register minor sensations. Do your best to roleplay accordingly.

Secondary Effect (DV17 - raised to 18, the first time ReTox is ingested)

  • If the user wasn’t already addicted to ReTox, they are now. While addicted, they crit fail on 2s and 3s, unless the user is currently experiencing the primary effect of ReTox (fails on 2s remain, fails on 3s are temporarily removed).

  • While addicted to ReTox, you will have trouble feeling any physical sensations but the most severe, your sense of taste, touch, and smell will be greatly diminished, and you’ll periodically experience severe depressive episodes unless under the effects of the drug. It isn’t the drug itself that you crave, but that feeling of sensation. Do your best to roleplay accordingly.
2d6 damage unless Resist Torture/Drugs. Armor does not apply.
A high-impact grenade launcher. You can use a variety of grenades with this.

Deals no Damage. Hit targets with Meat eyes roll DV15 Resist Torture/Drugs Check. On fail suffer Damaged Eye Critical Injury (See pg. 188) and can no longer speak for 1 minute. Does not deal critical bonus damage.
Thermite rounds bury themselves into a target and briefly ignite. Add +1 damage to the attack. If a 1 is rolled while firing, the weapon jams.

A basic lob or pitch of an object. Use an Action to make a Ranged Attack at up to 25m/yds. Targets can choose to Dodge your throw if they can Dodge bullets. Melee weapons deal their base damage, but don't penetrate half SP.

information.notes:
Mook Tier - Tier 5/Org Boss - High-level named threats who hold significant hard power, in charge of leading the various orgs found around NC.

Gang-Wide Ability - Circus Freaks: Bozos have the uncanny ability to sus out people’s deepest fears courtesy of aftermarket interrogation soft, be it for the addictive thrill-seeking of merrymakers, or the twisted torture of the joyboys. When a Bozo enters a scene, every player must make a Willpower Save (roll 1d10 - a result less or equal to the player’s Willpower stat passes). On a failure, that player immediately loses a facedown check against every Bozo in the scene. This penalty is only removed once all Bozos are killed, rendered unconscious, or removed from a scene.

Additionally, most Bozos will enter combat under the influence of ReTox, the gang’s drug of choice. See the item description for more information.

Extra Gear – Choose between a Bozo Confetti Flamethrower which has been modified to spray chemicals instead of shotgun shells (same area of effect; loaded with Vials of Biotoxin, Poison, or other aerosolized drugs; 1 dose of a drug = 4 shots, the target rolls resist drugs/torture instead of evasion when determining its effect after firing) or a Bozo Noisemaker Grenade Launcher and 4 grenades of the GM’s choice. Any explosions are accompanied by sound effects and bright colors.

Ability - Playing To Play, Not To Win: A joker can dodge bullets using their physical skill. Additionally, when wielding their knife, damage rolls of 5s and 6s which damage through armor can also inflict critical injuries.

Jokers are exclusively motivated by their passions, which is reflected by their ruthless disregard for the well being of themselves and their allies. When a joker rolls a d10, before seeing the result, you may add up to 6 to the roll. After doing so, distribute ½ that amount of luck to any players which are below their starting amount (if no players are, notify them so they can start using it). Any players affected by Circus Freaks may not receive luck in this way.
